title: Scenario
description: Contract for a testkit catalog scenario (expected-result.json): the workflow to drive, the ordered human gate responses, and the assertions to project onto the captured run. The runtime loaders parse scenarios leniently; this schema is the test-only validation gate.

^_.description: Underscore-prefixed keys (e.g. _note) are author comments; ignored by the lenient loader.

type.description: The human-gate verb. input/review/stepApproval/escalation resume the standard pauses; toolApprove/toolReject resolve an AWAITING_TOOL_APPROVAL pause; toolContinue/toolRetry resolve an AWAITING_TOOL_DECISION pause; collection drives an AWAITING_COLLECTION pause through an ordered op list.

reason.description: Rejection reason recorded for a toolReject gate; ignored by the other gate types, which use note.

toolInvocationId.description: Optional explicit pending tool-invocation id for a tool gate; when omitted the harness auto-targets the run's single current pending invocation (failing closed when there is not exactly one).

ops.description: Ordered collection-gate operations applied at one AWAITING_COLLECTION pause (for a collection gate).

payloadRef.description: Inline JSON payload for a submit/replace op.

submissionId.description: For replace/withdraw: the 0-based ordinal of the originating submit in this op list (runtime ids are non-deterministic, so the harness maps the ordinal to the real id).

reason.description: Close reason (for a close op). OVERRIDE is a derived outcome CloseRequest rejects as a requestable reason, so it is not offered here; set override:true instead.

override.description: Close despite an unmet minimum (for a close op).

actorId.description: Acting actor for submit/replace/withdraw; when omitted the harness defaults to its own actor. Scripting distinct actors across ops exercises multi-submitter collections and owner-scoped replace/withdraw denial.
